... [5 lines omitted] ...

233→ ctx.git.commit.add_all(state.cwd) 234→ ctx.git.commit.commit(state.cwd, "WIP: Prepare for PR submission") 235→ return state 236→ 237→ 238→def capture_existing_pr_body(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: 239→ """Capture full PR body before gt submit overwrites it.""" 240→ if not state.quiet: 241→ click.echo(click.style(" Checking for existing PR...", dim=True)) 242→ pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name) 243→ if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound): 244→ return state 245→ if not pr_result.body: 246→ return state 247→ return dataclasses.replace(state, existing_pr_body=pr_result.body) 248→ 249→ 250→def push_and_create_pr(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: 251→ """Push branch and create/find PR. 252→ 253→ Dispatches internally: Graphite-first vs core path. 254→ """ 255→ # Use cached Graphite check from prepare_state instead of re-checking 256→ graphite_handles_push = ( 257→ state.use_graphite 258→ and state.graphite_is_authed is True 259→ and state.graphite_branch_tracked is True 260→ ) 261→ 262→ if graphite_handles_push:
... [15 lines omitted] ...

725→ 726→def finalize_pr(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: 727→ """Update PR title/body with footer, add labels, amend local commit, clean up diff file.""" 728→ if state.skip_description: 729→ return state 730→ click.echo(click.style("Phase 6: Updating PR metadata", bold=True)) 731→ 732→ if state.pr_number is None: 733→ return SubmitError( 734→ phase="finalize_pr", 735→ error_type="no_pr_number", 736→ message="No PR number available for finalization", 737→ details={}, 738→ ) 739→ 740→ pr_body = state.body or "" 741→ pr_title = state.title or "Update" 742→ 743→ # Use pre-captured PR body (captured before gt submit overwrites it) 744→ existing_pr_body = state.existing_pr_body 745→ 746→ # Check learn plan label 747→ impl_dir = resolve_impl_dir(state.cwd, branch_name=state.branch_name) 748→ is_learn_origin = is_learn_plan(impl_dir) if impl_dir is not None else False 749→ 750→ # Recover plan-header if missing from existing PR body 751→ recovered_header = None 752→ header_missing = not has_metadata_block(existing_pr_body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER) 753→ if header_missing and state.plan_context is not None: 754→ recovered_header = recover_plan_header(
... [35 lines omitted] ...

783→ 784→ # Add learn skip label if applicable 785→ if is_learn_origin: 786→ add_labels_resilient( 787→ ctx.github, 788→ time=ctx.time, 789→ repo_root=state.repo_root, 790→ pr_number=state.pr_number, 791→ labels=(ERK_SKIP_LEARN_LABEL,), 792→ ) 793→ 794→ # Amend local commit with title and body (without metadata footer) 795→ commit_message = pr_title 796→ if pr_body: 797→ commit_message = f"{pr_title}\n\n{pr_body}" 798→ ctx.git.commit.amend_commit(state.repo_root, commit_message) 799→ 800→ # Fix Graphite tracking divergence caused by the amend 801→ if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None: 802→ ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name) 803→ 804→ # Clean up temp diff file 805→ if state.diff_file is not None and state.diff_file.exists(): 806→ try: 807→ state.diff_file.unlink() 808→ except OSError as e: 809→ if state.debug: 810→ click.echo(click.style(f" Failed to clean up diff file: {e}", dim=True)) 811→ 812→ click.echo(click.style(" PR metadata updated", fg="green"))

Perfect! All files have been updated successfully. Let me create a summary of the changes made:
## Summary
I've successfully added the two new fields `graphite_is_authed` and `graphite_branch_tracked` to all `SubmitState` constructor calls in the test files under `tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/`. Here's what was updated:
### Files Updated with `graphite_is_authed=None, graphite_branch_tracked=None`:
1. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_capture_existing_pr_body.py** 2. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_cleanup_impl_for_submit.py** 3. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_commit_wip.py** 4. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_core_submit_flow.py** (uses `use_graphite=False` in most tests, so None is appropriate) 5. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_extract_diff.py** 6. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_fetch_plan_context.py** 7. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_finalize_pr.py** 8. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_generate_description.py** 9. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_link_pr_to_objective_nodes.py** 10. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_prepare_state.py** (correctly set to None since prepare_state populates these fields)
### Files Updated with `graphite_is_authed=True, graphite_branch_tracked=True`:
11. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_enhance_with_graphite.py** - Set to True because these tests verify the enhance_with_graphite function, which requires Graphite to be authenticated and the branch to be tracked 12. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_graphite_first_flow.py** - Set to True because the graphite first flow is only entered when Graphite is enabled and the branch is tracked
### Additional Assertion Updates:
13. **/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_run_pipeline.py** - Added assertions to verify the new fields are properly initialized in `make_initial_state`: - `assert state.graphite_is_authed is None` - `assert state.graphite_branch_tracked is None`
... [2 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
I see a problem with `test_existing_pr_adds_footer_if_missing` (line 298). This test expects the footer to be added to an existing PR with body "Some PR body". But now `_core_submit_flow` uses `state.existing_pr_body` instead of making a `get_pr` call. The test creates the state with `existing_pr_body=""` (default), but the PR body is "Some PR body". The `capture_existing_pr_body` step would have captured this in the real pipeline, but since `_core_submit_flow` is tested in isolation, I need to pass `existing_pr_body` in the state.
Let me fix this test:

I need to add `remote_urls` to the `FakeGit` in this test. Let me check what `FakeGit.get_remote_url` does when no remote_urls are set:

session.assistant:
I notice the `_make_state` in `test_prepare_state.py` has `use_graphite=False` by default. This is correct because `prepare_state` only checks graphite auth when `use_graphite` is True. All existing tests pass `use_graphite=False`, so the graphite caching code won't be exercised by the existing tests. The existing tests should still pass as-is.
Now let me check the `test_run_pipeline.py` for the `make_initial_state` test:

session.assistant:
Plan #8786 implementation is complete. PR submitted at https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8786.
## Summary of changes
**3 optimizations to reduce redundant API calls in `erk pr submit`:**
1. **Eliminated redundant API calls in `finalize_pr`** — Removed the `get_pr_for_branch` call at the end (PR URL was already in `state.pr_url`) and the `check_auth_status` + `get_graphite_url` block (graphite URL was already set by earlier steps).
2. **Used `state.existing_pr_body` for footer check** — In `_core_submit_flow`, replaced the `get_pr` call for checking the footer with the pre-captured `state.existing_pr_body` from the `capture_existing_pr_body` step. Also replaced the `get_pr` call after `create_pr` (for getting URL) with URL construction from repo info.
3. **Cached Graphite auth and branch tracking in `SubmitState`** — Added `graphite_is_authed` and `graphite_branch_tracked` fields populated once in `prepare_state`. Used these cached values in `push_and_create_pr` (replacing `should_enhance_with_graphite` which re-checked auth + branches) and `enhance_with_graphite` (replacing direct `check_auth_status` + `get_all_branches` calls).
**Net reduction:** ~4 redundant GitHub/Graphite API calls eliminated per submit, removing the `should_enhance_with_graphite` import entirely.
